title: With Amit Shah by his side, Bihar CM Nitish Kumar files nomination for Rajya Sabha
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar filed his Rajya Sabha nomination today, accompanied by Union Home Minister Amit Shah. This move follows his decision to step down and head to Parliament, fulfilling a long-held aspiration. The transition, occurring after a recent election victory, signals a potential shift in Bihar's leadership within the NDA.

title: Trump's gold coup: Venezuela to ship a ton of bullion to US
content: Venezuela's state-owned mining company, Minerven, has agreed to sell up to 1,000 kilograms of gold dore bars to commodities trader Trafigura, with the gold destined for US markets. This multimillion-dollar deal, involving 98% final gold content, highlights strengthening US-Venezuela commercial ties under the Trump administration's supervision.

title: Who killed Nancy Grewal? Family in Punjab alleges conspiracy after influencer’s murder in Canada
content: The mother of murdered Punjabi influencer Nancy Grewal alleges three individuals are responsible for her daughter's death in Canada. Grewal, known for her outspoken social media commentary on religious and political figures, had reportedly received threats and faced a prior house fire. Her mother believes a local gurdwara figure is the main conspirator.

title: Amazon lays off employees in division that VP said remains 'strategic priority'
content: Amazon has laid off at least 100 employees in its robotics division—the unit that designs and builds automation systems for its warehouse network. The cuts are the latest in a cost-cutting campaign that has eliminated more than 57,000 corporate roles since late 2022, following a pandemic-era hiring spree. The reductions come as Amazon shelves its Blue Jay warehouse robot after less than six months and pivots toward a new modular warehouse system called Orbital.

title: What happens if India vs England semi-final washes out? ICC rules explained
content: India face England in the T20 World Cup semi-final on Thursday, with a reserve day in place by the ICC.  A minimum of 10 overs per side is needed for a result. If the match is entirely washed out, England, having topped their Super 8 group, would advance over India, who finished second in their Super 8 group.

title: What is Minuteman III missile? The 'doomsday' weapon US tested amid war with Iran
content: The United States conducted a test of its nuclear-capable Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missile off the California coast. This unarmed missile launch, from Vandenberg Space Force Base, aimed to verify the effectiveness, readiness, and accuracy of the system. The test occurred amidst ongoing strikes in the Middle East.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: 'Desi Maduro model, political abduction': Reactions pour in over Nitish's Rajya Sabha plan
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar confirmed his Rajya Sabha nomination, sparking political uproar. Opposition leaders criticized the move, calling it a "leadership coup" and "political abduction" orchestrated by the BJP, betraying the people's mandate. Kumar assured his commitment to Bihar's development remains unchanged.

title: 'Games on black soil': Finn Allen reveals the secret behind NZ’s T20 WC surge
content: Finn Allen's record-breaking century powered New Zealand into the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final. Allen credited their prior tour of India for adapting to the challenging black-soil pitches, enabling their dominant chase against South Africa.  He also lauded the bowlers for setting up the victory and praised his opening partner Tim Seifert's form.

title: Petrol and diesel prices: What does Middle East crisis mean for fuel cost? Check list
content: Despite escalating Middle East tensions and a surge in global crude oil prices, petrol and diesel rates in major Indian cities remain steady. Government sources indicate a strategy to shield consumers, with oil marketing companies absorbing some of the price hike. This approach aims to prevent significant inflation and GDP impact, ensuring stability for the public.

title: South Korea sends 'Iran warning' to Samsung and other chipmakers
content: South Korea has warned its semiconductor giants, including Samsung and SK Hynix, about potential disruptions from the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Concerns are high over sourcing critical chip-making materials like helium from the Middle East. This warning arrives as the industry already grapples with AI-driven supply bottlenecks, and broader tech risks emerge for Middle Eastern AI data center expansion.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Injury Update: Will Thunder Star Play vs Knicks?
content: Oklahoma City's star guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's availability against the New York Knicks on March 4 is the key question. Dealing with an abdominal strain, his potential return could significantly impact the game. Despite injuries, the Thunder boast a strong 48-15 record, while the Knicks are 40-22 and formidable at home.

title: Chhattisgarh forest guard seen with woman companion thrashed, made to dance by mob
content: A forest guard in Surajpur was allegedly manhandled and forced to dance by local youths after being seen with a woman friend. Video clips of the incident, which occurred a week ago, have surfaced online, highlighting concerns about rising vigilantism. The forest department is awaiting the guard's formal response to initiate legal action.

title: Draymond Green reacts to Blackpink K-Pop song mentioning his name
content: Golden State Warriors star Draymond Green expressed excitement after being name-dropped by the popular Korean girl group Blackpink. It was in their new song "Me and My." Green, a four-time NBA champion, views the K-Pop mention as a significant validation of his global influence and popularity. He plans to listen to the song with his daughters, who are fans of K-Pop.

title: Connor McDavid sends clear message as Oilers face key trade deadline
content: Connor McDavid quickly endorsed the Oilers’ trade for Connor Murphy, stressing the need to add talent without subtracting key pieces. With Edmonton firmly in a win now phase and draft picks holding less value, the move reflects urgency around the Stanley Cup chase. Chicago gains a future asset, while the Oilers strengthen their blue line for an immediate playoff push.

title: 'My body has started sending me bills for my 20s': Redditor shares his wake-up call
content: A viral Reddit post reveals how neglecting health in one's 20s leads to physical "bills" in the 30s.  Young professionals are experiencing back pain, stiff necks, and aching joints due to poor sleep, bad posture, and improper exercise.  Experts confirm this accumulated damage is reversible with consistent effort and lifestyle changes.

title: ‘Nirantar’ Nitish turns a new page, set for Rajya Sabha after decades as Bihar CM
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is stepping down after leading the NDA to a significant victory, opting to move to the Rajya Sabha. This marks a major political shift for the veteran leader, opening the door for the BJP to potentially appoint its own chief minister in the state.

title: ‘Not a choke, a bloody walloping’: Coach’s brutal honesty after SA’s crushing loss
content: South Africa's T20 World Cup journey ended abruptly with a nine-wicket semifinal loss to New Zealand. Coach Shukri Conrad described the defeat as a 'bloody walloping' rather than a 'choke,' highlighting New Zealand's dominant performance. Finn Allen's record-breaking century sealed the emphatic win, leaving the Proteas stunned despite a proud tournament run.

title: Maruti 800 converted to Lamborghini! Jharkhand man's 'Jugaad' goes viral
content: A Jharkhand mechanic has transformed a Maruti 800 into a Lamborghini replica for approximately Rs 4 lakh. Mohammad Arif hand-crafted the modifications, including fiberglass panels, a wide stance, and a neon green finish. The custom build, featuring a two-seater interior and sunroof, has gained significant attention online.

title: Russian crude to rescue! Ships carrying Russia’s oil head to India amid supply shock
content: Ship-tracking data indicates that two Russian crude shipments which were originally bound for East Asia have redirected their course to India. With its energy security priorities in mind, India has sought to buy Russian crude to avoid supply shortages as Middle East tensions show no sign of abating.

title: Why did the boy who earned Rs 64 on a food delivery app is inspiring so many people?
content: A young Blinkit delivery worker, Manish Aswar, gained viral attention not for his earnings, but for his dignified approach to his first day. He shared his modest ₹65 income with pride and later celebrated his newfound online recognition by feeding stray dogs, highlighting the value of honest work.

title: West Asia conflict: 80 students among 111 Pune residents flown back from UAE
content: Over 100 Pune residents, including students, have safely returned from Dubai following the US-Israel-Iran conflict. A total of 184 were stranded, with efforts underway for the remaining travelers. Special flights arranged by Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde also brought back 164 tourists from Maharashtra. Authorities are monitoring the situation and advising vigilance.

title: Who is Auston Matthews’ new girlfriend Vanessa?
content: Auston Matthews played a crucial role in Team USA’s gold medal victory at the 2026 Winter Olympics, scoring three goals and adding four assists during the tournament. After years of criticism in Toronto, the Leafs captain answered doubters on the international stage. Off the ice, Matthews confirmed his relationship with Toronto skincare specialist Vanessa Piunno, who publicly celebrated their romance on Instagram. With Olympic gold secured and personal stability emerging, Matthews appears to be entering a defining new chapter in both his career and life.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
content: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.

title: 'Didn't start war, but we're finishing it': US releases video of first '100 hours' of war in Iran
content: US Central Command released a video detailing the first 100 hours of Operation Epic Fury, a joint US-Israeli military campaign targeting Iran's security apparatus. The operation, which began after strikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader, has involved over 2,000 strikes and significantly reduced Iranian drone and missile launches.

title: T20 World Cup: How Shivam Dube became India’s man for clutch moments
content: Shivam Dube has consistently delivered crucial performances for India in T20 cricket's high-pressure moments. From the T20 World Cup final to the Asia Cup, his timely contributions have steered the team to victory.  Dube's batting momentum is building, and his impact, even in brief cameos, is vital for India's success, though his bowling requires improvement.

title: China boosts military budget by 7%, aims to carry out 'major defence-related projects'
content: China announced a 7% defense budget increase for 2026, reaching $276.8 billion to bolster military capabilities and assert claims over Taiwan and the South China Sea. This move, part of a steady modernization of the PLA, aims to counter US influence and ensure diplomatic independence, though it raises regional concerns.

title: China sets GDP growth goal at less than 5%! Trump tariffs, domestic troubles weigh
content: The GDP growth target for 2026 appeared in a draft of the government’s yearly work report. Although the range broadly matches market expectations, it is the first reduction since officials adjusted the goal to around 5% in 2023.

title: ‘Missile struck 4.5km away’: Techie shares real-time Middle East updates from Dubai
content: A Zepto software developer, Kritika Kumari, was stranded in Dubai amidst escalating Middle East conflict. Facing canceled flights and missile strikes, she and colleagues successfully navigated a complex journey via buses and taxis to Muscat, Oman. From there, they secured flights back to Bengaluru, highlighting the scramble for safety among Indians caught in the crossfire.

title: MBBS student killed, 3 injured as speeding Fortuner rams group of bikers in UP
content: Tragedy struck Gorakhpur as a speeding Fortuner claimed the life of a 22-year-old medical student and injured three others on the Mohaddipur Overbridge. The devastating collision occurred Wednesday night. Police have arrested the driver, Golden Sahni, and seized the vehicle. A case of culpable homicide has been registered, with authorities vowing strict action.

title: Got $5,000 in cash, family under 'threat': Pak man accused of plotting to kill Trump points to Iran
content: A Pakistani national, Asif Merchant, has been charged by the US Justice Department for allegedly plotting to assassinate President Trump and Joe Biden in 2024.  Merchant claims an Iranian handler instructed him, and he was apprehended after attempting to hire undercover FBI agents posing as hitmen.  He cited threats to his family in Iran as his motive.

title: Morgan Stanley lays off 2,500 employees: Who got impacted? Check details
content: Financial giant Morgan Stanley has reduced its workforce by 2,500 employees. This move impacts investment banking, trading, and wealth management. Financial advisors remain unaffected. The company achieved record revenue in 2025 and saw strong profits. Executives were optimistic for 2026. Job cuts are attributed to strategy and performance. This follows a trend of companies streamlining operations.

title: Fresh controversy hits Pak: T20 WC player fined over misconduct with female hotel staff
content: A Pakistan T20 World Cup squad member was reportedly fined for misbehaving with a female hotel staff member in Kandy, Sri Lanka. The incident occurred before the team's final Super Eight match, with the player expected to face further disciplinary action from the Pakistan Cricket Board upon their return home.

title: Fact Check: Will Sanju Samson miss T20 World Cup semi-final vs England?
content: Speculation arose on social media regarding Sanju Samson facing disciplinary action for his emotional on-field celebration after India's win. However, ICC rules suggest such an act, if reviewed, would likely be a Level 1 offense, attracting fines or demerit points, not a ban. Former umpire dismissed the rumors, stating it was an emotional celebration, not equipment abuse.

title: No issue can be resolved through military conflict: PM Modi
content: Mr. Modi made the remarks after holding wide-ranging talks with Finnish President Alexander Stubb, who is on a four-day visit to India

title: Karnataka ready to implement third phase of Upper Krishna irrigation project but BJP MPs are scared of PM, says Minister M. B. Patil
content: Minister says BJP MPs from Karnataka are afraid to approach Prime Minister Narendra Modi with a request to issue a gazette notification to enable the State Government to go ahead with the work

title: India, China top Asia-Pacific region with highest metabolic diseases burden
content: In 2023, India had approximately 21 million DALYs and 5.8 lakh deaths from type 2 diabetes. High systolic BP caused nearly 3.8 crore DALYs and about 15.7 lakh deaths, Anoop Misra, one of the co-authors of the study said

title: Jyoti Waghmare: Shinde Sena’s pick for Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra
content: Ms. Waghmare joined the party in April 2023 following the Shiv Sena split, and was appointed as the State spokesperson and the district contact chief for Dharashiv

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CM Stalin expresses happiness over DMK-Congress seat-sharing deal
content: Participating in the marriage of School Education Minister, Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi’s relative at Thanjavur, Mr. Stalin said he had come after having successfully completing the seat-sharing deal which has been “appreciated by all”.

title: Iran-Israel war poses near-term challenges to Indian economy: RBI MPC member
content: “In the present scenario, a hike in oil prices, exports disruptions and impact on remittances have been identified as the immediate challenges,” RBI MPC member Nagesh Kumar said

title: Nitish Kumar’s political journey: From rebellion to longest-serving CM
content: Nitish Kumar has navigated the complex political terrain of Bihar, marked by strategic alliances and governance initiatives aimed at promoting inclusivity and development

title: India rejects claims of U.S. using its ports against Iran
content: The Ministry of External Affairs dismisses claims that the U.S. was using India’s ports to launch strikes against Iran, calling them ‘fake and false’

title: As Nitish Kumar eyes Rajya Sabha seat, political buzz over change in Bihar leadership mounts
content: All eyes are on Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, amid indications that he may soon enter active politics

title: Rajya Sabha election: DMDK nominates treasurer L.K. Sudhish as candidate
content: The move will give the party founded by late actor Vijayakant its first representation in the Upper House

title: Curbs on movement, assembly remain in force in Kashmir after protests against Khamenei's killing
content: The restrictions were imposed on March 2, after spontaneous protests broke out across Kashmir against the killing of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in U.S.-Israel joint strikes

title: Tamil Nadu restricts private schools from conducting political, ideological, communal events
content: The government has amended the Tamil Nadu Private Schools (Regulation) Rules, 2023, and has included clauses to restrict activities by any external person, association, or organisation

title: Comfort fit for conservancy workers: how three Indian cities are getting the ‘measurements’ right
content: What does it take to design gloves, masks and uniform that factor in the various challenges faced by workers on the waste beat? Indore, Bengaluru and Pune have an answer

title: Three suspended Congress MLAs join BJP ahead of Assembly elections in Assam
content: Union Minister of State Pabitra Margherita said, "Leaders and functionaries from other parties are joining the BJP today. They are showing their support for our ideology"

title: With Congress’s Rajya Sabha picks from Telangana, Revanth Reddy strengthen’s his grip
content: Congress is fielding senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi and party strategist Vem Narender Reddy from Telangana

title: Ships anchored off Gulf coasts after Strait of Hormuz blaze; Indian crew members express concern
content: A Chief Engineer from Hyderabad, currently aboard an anchored ship, noted that the vessel he is on has been stationary for days

title: Ahead in health, but a new set of challenges awaits Tamil Nadu
content: At the heart of Tamil Nadu’s successes in healthcare lies a dedicated public health system — a wide, well-distributed network of primary health centres.

title: ITF W35 women’s tennis tournament Kalaburagi: Ankita Raina and Zeel Desai advance to second round, but local favourite Soha Sadiq bows out
content: Local favourite Soha Sadiq thrilled the home crowd with a strong start against Singapore’s Eva Marie Desvignes, but could not advance to the next round

title: Two bodies recovered after fire breaks out in Delhi's Paharganj building
content: Twenty-five fire tenders were rushed to the spot after receiving the call, the Delhi Fire Service officer said

title: How U.S.-Israel attack on Iran could impact millions of Indian migrant workers
content: UAE, where Iranian missiles have landed targeting U.S. bases, is the second largest source of India’s inward remittances and also a large hub for Indian migrant workers engaged in blue-collar jobs

title: Transport operators in Maharashtra set for Statewide strike from Thursday; demand rollback of e-challan system
content: At the heart of the agitation is a slew of grievances, with the administration of e-challans emerging as the primary flashpoint

title: Nurses to continue strike in Thiruvananthapuram
content: UNA, which called the strike, represents the majority of nurses working in as many as 490 private hospitals, including clinics, in the State

title: Five women achievers honoured by Rajalakshmi Engineering College
content: The awards were given away by Thangam Meganathan, Chairperson, Rajalakshmi Institutions, in the presence of Tejshree Abhay Meganathan, Executive Director, Rajalakshmi Institutions

title: CM Stalin announces solatium to families of four persons killed in Gingee car accident
content: Four persons from Chennai were killed when the car they were travelling in fell into a roadside well at Alampoondi near Gingee on Tuesday

title: Sci-tech projects from 17 schools and 60 college groups showcased at Anna University on National Science Day
content: Quiz, drawing, and essay competitions were also organised where around 200 students from 30 schools participated

title: Ceramic industry in Gujarat's Morbi stares at shutdown as West Asia conflict disrupts gas supply
content: Petroleum and gas shipments coming from Gulf countries have been impacted, with consignments stuck at the Strait of Hormuz, which is under Iran's control

title: The SIR conundrum in West Bengal
content: While the Trinamool Congress under the leadership of Mamata Banerjee used SIR as an electoral opportunity and used the harassment of people to target the ECI and BJP led government at the Centre, the West Bengal BJP leadership appears to be disappointed with the outcome of the exercise as deletions in the electoral roll are far less than what they had wished for.

title: Excise detects rampant use of banned tobacco products by bus crew
content: The department booked over 30 cases under the Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products Act during an intensive drive held in district

title: Internal reservation: Dalit Ministers hold meetings to arrive at a consensus ahead of Karnataka Cabinet meeting on March 5
content: Ahead of the Cabinet meeting during which the internal reservation issue is scheduled to be discussed, Dalit Ministers held parleys to arrive at a possible broader consensus on the issue that has now driven a wedge among Scheduled Castes and left the Cabinet divided

title: DMK-Congress deal: the leaders who were instrumental in breaking the stalemate
content: Sustained efforts of Congress national president Mallikarjun Kharge, former Union Minister P. Chidambaram, DMK Parliamentary Party leader Kanimozhi, and Congress MP Karti P. Chidambaram helps the two parties reach an agreement on seat sharing

title: Tank of centuries-old temple in Thiruneermalai set to be restored by April, say officials
content: 50% of work on the tank at Sri Ranganathaperumal Temple near Chromepet over; concrete laying work and removal of silt under way, compound wall to be built, CCTV cameras to be installed

title: KGMCTA defers strike for a week following Kerala government assurances
content: Government agrees to disburse the pay revision arrears of 18 months immediately. Arrears of the remaining 39 months will be disbursed in accordance with legal advice

title: Kalamassery municipality mulls energy-efficient alternatives for street lights
content: The municipality has drafted a project to replace tube lights with LED lights in the first phase of the project expected to cost around ₹75 lakh

title: Applications open for IISER Aptitude Test 2026
content: Corrections in application forms will be carried out between April 16 and 18 and exam hall tickets will be released on May 24

title: CM Stalin inspects ongoing construction of elevated corridor on Anna Salai
content: The Chief Minister, along with the Minister for Public Works E.V. Velu and Minister for Health and Family Welfare Ma. Subramanian, inspected the 3.2 kilometre-long elevated road being constructed at a cost of ₹621 crore

title: Four workers faint after inhaling gas in effluent treatment plant in Ranipet
content: Initial inquiry revealed that the four workers had opened the tank, which is around 10 feet deep, to remove sludge

title: New Ranipet SP Sibin assumes charge
content: According to a press release, Mr. Sibin served as Superintendent of Police (SP) for Chengalpattu district

title: Textiles Minister inaugurates new Bypass Road in Ranipet
content: The new Bypass Road is funded under the Comprehensive Road Infrastructure Development Programme 2024-25

title: CBI launches probe into Puducherry counterfeit drugs case
content: The Central agency took over the case after investigation by local police revealed potential implications affecting public health, economic security, and national interest

title: Khazana Jewellery’s 268kg gold stock case: Madras High Court directs income tax settlement commission to reconsider settlement plea
content: Chief Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava and Justice G. Arul Murugan hold that the commission had rejected the plea in 2020 without proper consideration

title: SIR protests continue in West Bengal days after publication of the draft rolls
content: With the Assembly election due in a few months, the TMC may seek to keep the SIR pot boiling; Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee plans to sit in a dharna in Kolkata against the SIR from March 6

title: No possibility of merger of NCP factions any more, our position is clear, says Jayant Patil
content: Jayant Patil says reunification talks had been initiated at Ajit Pawar’s insistence and are no longer on the table.

title: Palestinian envoy to India slams ‘jungle law’ in Iran-U.S. conflict
content: Abu Shawesh has expressed deep concern over statements from major powers indicating that the window for negotiations has been closed

title: Congress will name Rajya Sabha candidate when filing nomination: Himachal CM
content: With a clear majority in the 68-member Himachal Pradesh Assembly, the Congress's victory should be a foregone conclusion, but for its bitter experience in the previous Rajya Sabha election

title: 180 flights cancelled amid growing West Asia conflict
content: Air India Express announced that its air services to Muscat will remain operational; Air India says it will deploy additional flights to Toronto, Frankfurt and Paris to cater to the high demand

title: Watch: Government’s silence on the Iran strike and Khamenei’s death draws opposition criticism
content: Indian opposition leaders have strongly criticised the Narendra Modi government for its silence after coordinated strikes by the United States and Israel that reportedly killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Parties including Rahul Gandhi and Sonia Gandhi have said that not condemning the attack or expressing condolences undermines India’s credibility and raises questions about its foreign policy, urging a parliamentary debate and clearer articulation of India’s stance.

title: Trade bodies flag impact of West Asia crisis on trade-driven Karnataka
content: Many Kannadigas have their business set-ups in Jebel Ali or in other trade free zones in West Asia; many travelled through Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar or Doha to reach leisure or business destinations in the U.S., or Europe

title: Telangana CM, DGP meet Amit Shah to discuss impending surrender of top Maoist
content: Police deny such move and claim meeting was to brief the Union Home Minister about the naxalite movement and the requirements of the State police force

title: Foundation for ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel plant this month: CM
content: A shift from ‘Ease of Doing Business to Speed of Doing Business’ gave A.P. an edge over other States in attracting investments, says Chandrababu Naidu

title: Minister inaugurates rehabilitation colony for Ramayapatnam Port displaced families
content: Of the total cost of nearly ₹5,000 crore for the port, the State government has already spent ₹1,800 crore, and almost 77 per cent of the construction has been completed

title: Cochin Diocese’s post-SIR campaign seeks to ensure nobody loses right to vote
content: The diocese, comprising 40,750 families spread over four Assembly constituencies in two districts, has launched the drive to identify those who remain out of the electoral rolls even after the recently concluded Special Intensive Revision of voters’ list

title: Social activist flags frequent fires in Magadi taluk
content: Shabnam Hashmi, who runs Ajji’s Learning Centre, a non-profit education trust located in Jyothipalya village, said that since February 24, there have been three instances of forest fires.

title: Karnataka’s HPV vaccination plan revived under Centre’s national rollout
content: The programme announced in Karnataka earlier could not take off as planned due to delays in vaccine procurement

title: Before announcing W.B. poll dates, EC members to assess ground situation in the State from March 8
content: Full Commission to meet State poll, law enforcement officials, political parties; to check logistics, identify high tension areas for extra security deployment; plans for CCTV cameras outside poll booths

title: 90% data integration for Family Benefit Management System likely by April 30
content: FBMS is envisioned as a centralised digital platform to ensure transparent, targeted and seamless delivery of welfare benefits to eligible families across Andhra Pradesh

title: RCB matches at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium: Final clearance yet to come
content: The Karnataka State Cricket Association is required to comply with 17 major suggestions drafted by the expert committee to get final clearance; it has only complied with two so far

title: Gulf conflict: A.P. trying to evacuate stranded people in three days
content: Communication with Telugu people currently in Iran was yet to be fully established, and efforts were under way to reach out to them through diplomatic and community channels, says Minister Kondapalli Srinivas

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Ohio State, Miami create separation with key wins
content: The Buckeyes and Canes solidified their standings on the right side of the respective Big Ten and ACC bubbles.

title: Process over perfection: How UConn prepared to chase its third title in four years
content: The pressure of expectation derailed the Huskies' bid for a three-peat. Perspective has them back in contention.

title: Holtz, college football coaching great, dies at 89
content: Lou Holtz, the quick-witted college football coach who led Notre Dame to a national championship in 1988 and burnished his reputation as a master at rebuilding programs, has died at the age of 89.

title: Knicks' Brown: SGA tops in convincing refs of fouls
content: "SGA, he's a tough cover, and he does a great job of convincing the referees -- probably better than anybody in the league -- that he's getting hit," Knicks coach Mike Brown said after his team's 103-100 loss to the Thunder on Wednesday night.

title: Stewart's 32-point night lifts Mist to Unrivaled title
content: Despite a brilliant 40-point performance from Kelsey Plum, Breanna Stewart and Mist topped Phantom 80-74 in the championship game to become the queens of Unrivaled for 2026.

title: Browns' Garrett gets 9th speeding ticket since '17
content: Browns star Myles Garrett was issued a citation for speeding -- his ninth since entering the NFL in 2017 -- after he was clocked going 94 mph in a 70 mph zone.

title: S. Alabama-CCU brawl injures ref, sees 8 ejected
content: A brawl between South Alabama and Coastal Carolina in the women's Sun Belt Tournament on Wednesday left eight players ejected and knocked a referee to the ground where she required medical attention.

title: Rory: 'A shame' Rahm rejected DP World Tour deal
content: Rory McIlroy says it's "a shame" that Jon Rahm is the only LIV Golf player unwilling to accept the terms of a DP World Tour deal to eliminate future fines.

title: Brighton boss slams Arsenal tactics, tells PL to act
content: Brighton boss Fabian Hürzeler has launched a scathing attack on Arsenal's time-wasting tactics by calling on the Premier League to intervene.

title: Ready for NFL free agency? We have the latest buzz on active teams, player-team fits and more
content: Here's what you need to know about 2026 free agency. We picked out everything from players who might break the bank to sleepers to watch.

title: Trade grades: Did the Rams give up too much to acquire Trent McDuffie?
content: We're handing out report cards on offseason moves, including the Chiefs sending Trent McDuffie to the Rams.

title: Women's Bracketology: Bubble bursts for Bulldogs, Cardinal and Aggies
content: Losses Wednesday in their Champ Week openers dashed NCAA tournament hopes for three teams on the bubble.

title: Emma Hayes' USWNT vision for 2027 World Cup is becoming clear
content: After so much experimentation, how the USWNT will look under Emma Hayes at the 2027 Women's World Cup is becoming evident.

title: MLB Rank 2026: Ranking baseball's top 100 players
content: From star sluggers like Ohtani and Judge to top pitchers like Skubal, we rank -- and make predictions for -- the best players in baseball right now.

title: 'He wants a Cy Young': With his arm at full strength, Shohei Ohtani is a man on a mission
content: Dodgers teammates say MLB's most singular star has set his sights on another history-making achievement.

title: How Stewart and the Mist overcame Plum's 40 points to win the Unrivaled title
content: Despite 40 points from Kelsey Plum, Breanna Stewart led the Mist past the Phantom for the 2026 Unrivaled title.

content: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler had strong words for Arsenal's game management, but the Gunners now control their own destiny in the title race.

content: Morgan Gibbs-White and Elliot Anderson might have just dented Man City's chances of winning the league.

title: 2026 World Cup squads: Predicting starting XIs for England, Spain, France, more
content: With under 100 days to go until the 2026 World Cup, how do all the major contenders -- and the three co-hosts -- look in terms of squads and starters? We break down how things stand as the countdown heats up.

title: Chauncey Billups back in court: Why the Blazers coach is facing decades in prison

title: 💍 Tennis star Sabalenka announces engagement
content: INDIAN WELLS, CA -- Top-ranked tennis player  Aryna Sabalenka  has announced her engagement to Brazilian businessman Georgios Frangulis. Sabalenka posted a video of the proposal on Instagram, accompanied by the words "You & me, forever," along with a ring and heart emoji. A post shared by Aryna Sabalenka (@arynasabalenka) The news quickly drew congratulations from fellow tennis players, including  Carlos Alcaraz ,  Novak Djokovic ,  Ben Shelton ,  Caroline Wozniacki ,  Coco Gauff  and  Amanda Anisimova , who is a possible opponent for Sabalenka in the quarterfinals at the forthcoming Indian Wells tournament in the Southern California desert. U.S. women's rugby player Ilona Maher also sent her congratulations. The tournament, which opens on Wednesday, will be Sabalenka's first since she reached the Australian Open final, where she lost to  Elena Rybakina  6-4, 4-6, 6-4 on Jan. 31.
